page: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 672.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.0 GB of memory per core. The server includes 3891 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, offering high block storage performance. There are no GPUs bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 108527.62 and a Passmark CPU score of 81651.3. Cryptographic performance is top-tier, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 98760.0, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instructions. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 337.25 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 135.59 GB/sec for writes. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1701.0. Large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, yielding a poor 2.52 tokens/sec on a 70B model due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E112ibds_v5 offers high resource density and isolated hardware allocation, making it a viable option for enterprise workloads requiring dedicated physical resources without the overhead of bare metal. The combination of 672.0 GB of RAM, strong multi-threaded decompression, and high-speed local storage makes this server highly suitable for large relational databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 is an isolated virtual machine configured with 112 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 672.0 GB of RAM, and 3891 GB of local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 81651.3 and top-tier cryptographic execution. Memory bandwidth is highly robust, supporting uncached large-block reads at 337.25 GB/sec. While single-core processing and large-scale LLM text generation are limited, its high memory-to-core ratio and local storage capacity offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense environments. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 112 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3891 GB local storage, optimized for large databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 features 112 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3891 GB local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-capacity memory and storage platform designed for demanding enterprise databases and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory
3891 GB of high-performance local block storage
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution
Excellent memory bandwidth with 337.25 GB/sec uncached reads
Optimized for large relational databases and Redis caching layers
